Negative Impacts

Assurant (AIZ): Assurant provides risk management and specialty insurance solutions. While revenue rose, helped by higher premiums and investment income, shares faced short-term selling pressure along with the rest of the insurance space on fears that AI could accelerate disruption in the industry. The firm used the share price drop as an opportunity to boost its share repurchase program for the year.

Chevron Corporation (CVX): Not owning Chevron weighed on performance as the firm benefited from the sharp rally in crude prices.

Estee Lauder Companies Class A (EL): Shares of the leading manufacturer and marketer of prestigious beauty products detracted after the company announced a potential merger with Spanish fragrance company Puig.

1. Performance of individual stock evaluated is based on total effect. Total effect represents the opportunity cost of an investment manager's investment decisions relative to an overall benchmark, which can include holdings that are not held by the portfolio but contribute to relative portfolio performance.

Market Outlook

Equity market volatility in the first quarter started with concerns about how AI would disrupt and/or completely displace existing industries and companies; this was quickly overshadowed by the start of the war in Iran. In both cases, there is a high level of uncertainty and skepticism around the potential outcomes, and investors have largely responded by taking a more defensive posture. In our view, headline risk will remain elevated for the foreseeable future and will lead to more dramatic risk on/risk off days for the market.

We are the first to acknowledge that we do not have an edge on the macro environment, nor do we forecast potential geopolitical outcomes. What does give us confidence in these highly uncertain markets is our experienced portfolio management team, the depth and breadth of our resources as we make investment decisions, and a time-tested investment process designed to uncover opportunities in periods of uncertainty and elevated skepticism. One of the key pillars of our investment process is identifying companies with strong and improving fundamentals. In our view, companies with robust balance sheets, sustainable earnings and cash flow growth, and solid management teams should be able to withstand these types of environments better than their lower-quality peers.

We would also argue that the current environment calls for an active management approach. Equity markets are forward looking and can move quickly to price in, or price out, these types of risks. Investors who adopt too defensive a posture risk missing a potential quick recovery. We believe active managers with extensive resources at their disposal are best suited to identifying the winners and losers, regardless of the market driver. Above all, it is our view that investing in companies that sit at the nexus of strong and improving fundamentals, attractive valuations, and catalyst-driven business momentum has the potential to deliver the most attractive investment outcomes for our clients.

data.title: 4 To Watch Of 21 'Safer' April Dividends From 100 Fortune Best Companies To Work For (FBCTWF)
data.description:
Foreword

  • Fortune asserts: “The AI era has arrived, and every company must become a digital company in some capacity. What stands out among this year's 100 Best Companies to Work For is their ability to rebuild the social contract at work so people feel supported, trusted, and future-ready as they undergo that transition. For the 29th edition of our list, editorial partner Great Place to Work compiled confidential surveys from over 640,000 workers at eligible companies. Against the backdrop of the AI revolution, three themes define this year's top employers: They are listening more closely than their peers; they're investing heavily in AI-ready careers; and even as work becomes more digital and always-on, they're doubling down on analog perks that give employees more time to care for the people they love most.”

Any collection of stocks is more clearly understood when subjected to yield-based (dogcatcher) analysis. These 54 dividend paying Fortune Best Companies To Work For (FBCTWF) firms are perfect for the dogcatcher process. Here, April 20 data focused on the top 21 dividend payers. The full list of 100 is posted in the Afterword text at the tail of this article.

Unfortunately, of the 21 dividend-paying FBCTWF companies that live up to the Dogcatcher “safer” qualification showing free cash flow yields exceeding dividend yields, none of them pay dividend from $1k invested exceeding their single share prices,

The Dogcatcher (author) is forced to conclude that, while these companies care deeply about the future of their employees, the companies care not for their stockholding owners.

Actionable Conclusions (1-10): Analysts Estimated 19.49% To 52.67% Net Gains From Ten Top Fortune BCTWF To April, 2027

Four of the ten top Fortune BCTWF stocks by yield were also among the top ten gainers for the coming year based on analyst 1-year target prices. (They are tinted gray in the chart below). Thus, the yield-based forecast for these Fortune top dogs, as graded by Wall St. Wizards, was 40% accurate.

Estimated dividends from $1000 invested in each of the highest-yielding stocks plus the median of one-year analyst target prices, were counted. YCharts supplied the data points. Note: target prices from less than two analysts were not counted. Thus, ten probable April 20, 2027 net gainers were:

FBCTWF Top Ten April Dividend Dogs By Net Gains

Intuit Inc (INTU) was projected to net $526.68, based on the median of target price estimates from 34 analysts, plus annual dividend, less broker fees. The Beta number showed this estimate subject to risk/volatility 22% greater than the market as a whole.

Walker & Dunlop Inc (WD) was projected to net $388.80 based on the median of target estimates from 3 analysts, plus its latest dividend, less broker fees. The Beta number showed this estimate subject to risk/volatility 53% over the market as a whole.

Ryan Specialty Holdings Inc (RYAN) was projected to net $301.62 based on dividends, plus the median of target estimates from 19 brokers, less transaction fees. A Beta number was not available for RYAN.

Accenture (ACN) was projected to net $290.38 based on dividends, plus the median of target price estimates from 28 analysts, less broker fees. The Beta number showed this estimate subject to risk/volatility 24% over the market as a whole.

First American Financial Corporation (FAF) was projected to net $265.76, based on the median of target estimates from 5 analysts, plus dividends, less broker fees. The Beta number showed this estimate subject to risk/volatility 28% over the market as a whole.

Capital One Financial (COF) was projected to net $248.29, based on dividends, plus the median of target price estimates from 23 analysts, less broker fees. The Beta number showed this estimate subject to risk/volatility 15% over the market as a whole.

Stryker Corporation (SYK) was projected to net $220.98 based on dividends, plus the median of the target price estimates from 29 analysts, less broker fees. The Beta number showed this estimate subject to risk/volatility 8% under the market as a whole.

AbbVie Inc (ABBV) was projected to net $218.14 based on a median of target price estimates from 31 analysts, plus dividends, less broker fees. The Beta number showed this estimate subject to risk/volatility 62% under the market as a whole.

S&P Global Inc (SPGI) was projected to net $205.58 based on the median of target prices from 24 analysts, plus annual dividends, less broker fees. The Beta number showed this estimate subject to risk/volatility 21% over the market as a whole.

Salesforce Inc (CRM) was projected to net $194.90, based on dividends, plus the median of target price estimates from 9 analysts, less broker fees. A Beta number was not available for CRM.

The average net gain in dividend and price was estimated at 28.61% on $10k invested as $1k in each of these ten stocks. These gain estimates were subject to average risk/volatility 12% over the market as a whole.

The Dividend Dogs Rule

Stocks are dividend dogs when they show three key qualities: (1) paying reliable, repeating dividends, and (2) their prices fall. Therefore, (3) yield (dividend/price) grows higher than their peers. Thus, the highest yielding stocks in any collection become valued as ”dividend dogs." More specifically, they may be best-named "underdogs,” owing to their contrarian nature.

28 of 54 FBCTWF Dogs Show April 1Yr Total Returns

44 of 54 FBCTWF Dogs Show April Analyst Target Gains

36 of 54 FBCTWF Dogs Show Positive April Yields

Actionable Conclusions (11-20): 10 Top FBCTWF Dogs By Yield For April

Top-yielding ten April 2026 FBCTWF stocks represented seven of eleven Morningstar sectors.

One industrials representative placed first, Robert Half Inc (RHI) [1].

Four financials sector representatives placed second, third, seventh, and tenth, Walker & Dunlop [2], Alianz SE (ALIZF) [3], First American Financial Corporation [7], and MetLife Inc (MET) [10].

One real estate sector member placed fourth, Camden Property Trust (CPT) [4]. Then one materials representative placed fifth, Dow Inc (DOW) [5]. Thereafter the lone consumer staples representative placed sixth, Target Corporation (TGT) [6].

Finally, a health care stock claimed eighth place, AbbVie (ABBV) [8], and was followed by the lone technology representative in ninth, Accenture Plc (ACN) [9], to complete the top ten April list of Fortune Best Companies To Work For stocks by yield.

Fortune 54 Dividend Paying BCTWF Costs per Dividend Dollar as of April, 26-7

The above chart reveals 36 Fortune Best Companies To Work For subjected to the same test: divide the price of each stock by the annual dividend. Thus is calculated the cost per dividend dollar for each stock.

The least expensive dog of the list is Robert Half at $11.63 per dividend dollar and the most expensive is Nvidia (NVDA) at $5,050.51 per dividend dollar.

Actionable Conclusion (21): Four of Thirty-six April Dividend Paying FBCTWF Dogs Show Negative Free-Cash-Flow Margins or No Dividends

A negative gap between dividend yield and free cash flow yield defines a cash-poor stock. That is, their dividend payout exceeds their cash on hand to pay dividends. Four dividend paying FBCTWF stocks so infected are: Walker & Dylan, Camden Property, Dow, Ally, and Lennar. These are considered unsafe to buy.

Remember this dogcatcher yield-based stock-picking strategy is contrarian. That means rooting for (buying) the underdog is productive when you don't already own those stocks. If you do hold those stocks, then you must look for opportune pull-backs in price to add to your position to best improve your dividend yield. Plenty of pull-back opportunities appear to be ahead. Dread the Fed uncertainty, deals, wars, take-overs, and tariffs, too.

Price Drops or Dividend Increases Could Get Ten Top-Yield FBCTWF Dividend Dogs Back to "Fair-Price" Rates For Investors

The charts above retain the current dividend amount and adjust the share price to produce a yield (from $1K invested) to equal or exceed the single share price of each stock. As the top illustration shows, three in the top ten are already ideally priced. Besides those three, already in the ideal zone, four more top-yield stocks are within $61 of getting there.

The alternative, of course, could be that those companies raise their dividends or do stock splits. Those actions may be too much to ask in these highly disrupted, inflationary, recessionary, yet cash-rich, and volatile times. Mr. Market is far more effective at moving prices up or down to appropriate amounts, just watch and buy when your targeted stock price moves to the sweet spot.

Actionable Conclusions: (22-31) Broker 1-Yr. Targets Showed 19.49% To 52.45% Top Ten FBCTWF Upsides To April, 2027; (32) Four 0.15% to -6.37% Downsiders Trailed

To quantify top-dog rankings, analyst median price-target estimates provided a "market-sentiment" gauge of upside-potential. Added to the simple high-yield "dog" metrics, analyst median-price target-estimates provided another tool to dig-out bargains.

Analysts Forecast A 24.07% Advantage For 5 Highest-Yield, Lowest-Priced of 10 FBCTWF Stocks As of April, 2027

Ten-top FBCTWF Stocks were culled by yield for this monthly-update. Yield (dividend / price) results as verified by YCharts did the ranking.

As noted-above, top-ten FBCTWF Stocks stocks selected 4/20/25 by yield, represented seven of eleven sectors. Communications, consumer discretionary, energy, and utilities, missed the top-ten cut.

Actionable Conclusions: Analysts Expected 5 Lowest-Priced of Ten Highest-Yield Dividend FBCTWF Stocks (34) To Deliver 22.40% Vs. (35) 18.06% Net Gains by All Ten Come April 20, 2027

$5000 invested as $1k in each of the five lowest-priced stocks in the top ten top-ten FBCTWF analyst 1-year targets to deliver 24.07% more gain than from $5,000 invested in all-ten. The third lowest-priced top-ten yielding stock, Walker & Dunlop, showed top analyst-estimated gains of 38.88%.

The five lowest-priced top-yield FBCTWF dogs for April were: Robert Half; Dow; Walker & Dunlop; First American; MetLife, with prices ranging from $27.44 to $78.08.

Five higher-priced FBCTWF top-yield dogs as of April 20 were: Camden Property; Target; Accenture; AbbVie; Allianz, with prices ranging $103.40 to $445.21.

The distinction between five low-priced dividend dogs and the general field of ten reflected Michael B. O'Higgins' "basic method" for beating the Dow. The scale of projected gains based on analyst targets added a unique element of "market sentiment" gauging upside potential. It provided a here-and-now equivalent of waiting a year to find out what might happen in the market.

Caution is advised, however, since analysts are historically only 15% to 85% accurate on the direction of change and just 0% to 15% accurate on the degree of change.

As the Spring euphoria in the market cools and the feckless Fed-fueled slide and dithering presidential pronouncements gain momentum through 2026 with the exception of a few meme stocks, look for the half of the top ten “safer” FBCTWF to become Fair-priced… slowly, but surely, or extremely suddenly.

Afterword

Watch 5 of 21 ‘Safer' FBCTWF Stocks become IDEAL Dividend Dogs

Mid April 2026, 21 of the 54 FBCTWF Dividend stocks were tagged "safer" for dividends as they showed positive one-year returns and free-cash-flow-yields greater-than dividend-yields.

YCharts 4/20/26 data showed five of twenty-four on the cusp of becoming “IDEAL” as dividend payers with dividends from $1,000 invested exceeding their single share prices.

How 'Safer' Dividend Vs. Price Data Can Make "Fair Price” Results For Investors

The chart at the top above reveals five dividends from $1,000.00 invested in our top ten 'safer' FWMAC stocks within $61 of the dogcatcher standard of being equal to (or greater than) the price of a single share. Those five nearly ideal “safer” dogs were Hewlett Packard Enterprise, First American Financial, Synchrony Financial, Cisco Systems, and Merck.

The middle chart shows the recent dividend amount and the bottom chart adjusts that share price to produce a yield (from $1K invested) equal to or greater than the single share price of each stock; a so-called, “fair price.”

A market correction of 73% could put all ten share prices of these “safer” survivors above or equal to their dividend payouts from $1k invested.

There are, of course, other ways for dividends from $1K invested to exceed the price of a single share of stock. That would be for the boards of directors these companies to raise dividends or authorize stock splits to bring price and dividends the dogcatcher ideal level.
